# Clerk Auth
## Purpose
Provide a secure and consistent Clerk integration pattern for authentication, session handling, and B2B organization features.

## Inputs Required
- App routing model and protected surface areas.
- Identity requirements (consumer, B2B orgs, SSO readiness).
- Session strategy and token consumption points.
- Authorization model and role mappings.

## Step-by-Step Workflow
1. Configure Clerk app and environment keys per deployment target.
2. Integrate sign-in/up UI and middleware protection boundaries.
3. Map user and organization identities to application roles.
4. Implement server-side session and authorization checks.
5. Validate auth flows (login, logout, expired session, org switching).
6. Document operational setup and incident fallback steps.

## Validation Checks
- Unauthorized routes are properly protected.
- Session state is consistent across server and client boundaries.
- Organization context is enforced where required.
- Security-sensitive endpoints verify identity and permissions.

## Output Format
- Auth architecture summary.
- Route protection and role-mapping matrix.
- Flow test checklist with pass/fail status.

## Integration Hooks (which agents use this)
- `auth-security-manager`
- `frontend-framework-manager`

## Anti-Patterns
- Relying on client-only checks for protected operations.
- Mixing identity and authorization logic without clear boundaries.
- Skipping org-context enforcement in multi-tenant surfaces.

## External References (official docs URL from tech stack)
- https://clerk.com/docs
